title: Ny aktie med aggressiv vækstcase - er den en investering værd?
summary_md:
# Ny aktie med aggressiv vækstcase - er den en investering værd?

**Feed**: borsen-podcast  
**Published**: 2026-06-10  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A Danish investor podcast covering whether to invest in FIFA World Cup-themed stocks (concluding it's too late), the speculative SpaceX IPO, the aggressive Danish roll-up IPO of Installatørgruppen, and the guests' favourite holdings. The hosts are sceptical of hype-driven names (SpaceX, World Cup plays) but bullish on structural-growth and turnaround stories like CATL, Adidas, Vestas and electrification names. Overall mood is cautious and selective amid an unusually volatile, flow-driven market.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 ADS — Adidas | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Turnaround under ex-Pandora CEO, World Cup boost (sponsors 14 of 48 teams), analyst consensus ~15% above current price.

#### Reasoning

Lars-Jørgen holds it as a favourite and tactical allocation: a struggling brand in a turnaround that should get a sales lift from the World Cup as fans buy €75-100 national jerseys plus footwear. Analyst target ~€196 (~15% upside). He had similar success with Adidas four years ago. Peter notes it's not on his fund's structural-theme list but acknowledges the case is interesting.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the half-year report in late August disappoints on the turnaround
- if World Cup sales fail to materialise
- if the stock runs above analyst consensus targets

> *"Adidas är en av mina favoritaktier i öjeblik. Jag kör mycket på det jag menar till fotboll-VM."*

---

### ⛔ SPACEX — SpaceX | **AVOID** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 80%

IPO at $1.8T cap, ~$23bn negative FCF last year, prospectus admits it may never be profitable; FOMO/hype-driven.

#### Reasoning

Both Peter and Lars-Jørgen decline the IPO. The company burns enormous cash, had ~one year of runway at last year's burn rate, faces accelerating investment, and even the Starlink division is FCF-negative after investment. Valuation is ~$1,000bn above Morningstar fair value. Hosts view it as a short-term FOMO/hype trade, not a sound long-term investment as fundamentals aren't in order.

#### Sell Triggers

- if no path to earnings emerges
- if burn rate accelerates further without funding
- thesis is to avoid, so confirmation of profitability would reverse it

> *"Jag syns bara att risikoen är för stor med allt det. Det är fundamentalt att det inte är i orden."*

---

### 🟢 VWS — Vestas | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Strong order intake and improved operations on global renewables build-out; was the portfolio's best 2025 stock (+68%).

#### Reasoning

Lars-Jørgen holds Vestas for clients and personally, citing the structural need for more energy and renewables, good order intake (including US Republican states) and better operational control. Risks: Trump's anti-wind stance, oil-price swings, dilutive capital raises and weak solidity (~14-15% equity/balance, below his 20% comfort line). Recently fell ~15% in two weeks against logic, which he sees as opportunity.

#### Sell Triggers

- if further dilutive equity raises occur
- if order intake deteriorates
- if US policy further restricts onshore wind
- if solidity weakens further

> *"Jag håller fast i Vestas för det är ju för de medlemmar som har kommit i år med god orderingang."*

---

### 🟢 300750 — CATL | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 80%

World's largest battery maker (~45% share) at ~2.5-3% FCF yield, ~12% structural growth; Peter's fund took a large position.

#### Reasoning

Peter's concentrated fund bought CATL as its electrification idea, viewing it as a future 'Standard Oil' of the energy transition. 45% market share vs #2 at 10-15% gives exponential operating leverage; IP-licensing (Ford, Arm-like royalties), new fabs in Spain/Hungary/US, 15,000 battery researchers and fast-charging innovation. Solar is the most exponential energy source and batteries are essential to electrify society. 7-10 year hold.

#### Sell Triggers

- if China battery dominance is eroded by competitors
- if structural growth falls well below ~12%
- if geopolitical/listing access (Hong Kong) deteriorates

> *"Det blir förmodligen standard oil i den här framtiden med elektrificering."*

---

### ⛔ CARL-B — Carlsberg | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Structural pressure on beer/spirits from GLP-1 drugs and a generation drinking markedly less; tournament sales boost fading.

#### Reasoning

Peter is in the camp that sees little upside in Carlsberg (and brewers generally). A new generation's changed alcohol consumption plus GLP-1 weight-loss drugs will pressure beer/spirits volumes for years; he expects management to argue they can offset the erosion, but he is sceptical. World Cup beer sales are not enough to change the structural trajectory.

#### Sell Triggers

- if GLP-1 impact on alcohol consumption proves overstated
- if volumes stabilise in core markets
- if pricing offsets volume erosion

> *"Vi ser ett press på de bolag som cicad med öl och spirit i åren framöver."*

---

### 🟢 ALK-B — ALK-Abelló | **BUY** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Long-held favourite described as 'superb'; results due soon.

#### Reasoning

Lars-Jørgen names ALK-Abelló among his base/favourite holdings, calling it consistently excellent, with upcoming results a potential catalyst. Limited detailed reasoning given beyond a strong qualitative endorsement.

#### Sell Triggers

- if upcoming results disappoint

> *"en annan favoritaktie som jag också har är den där Alcabello. Den är också bara suverän."*

---

### 🟢 ABB — ABB | **BUY** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Base holding riding the electrification wave driven partly by data-centre demand.

#### Reasoning

Lars-Jørgen holds ABB as a 'crydded' base position to capture the electrification boom. Peter's fund also evaluated ABB (vs Schneider Electric and CATL) for electrification exposure before choosing CATL, indicating ABB is a credible but secondary pick in the theme.

#### Sell Triggers

- if electrification/data-centre capex slows
- if valuation overextends

> *"En annan utländsk aktie som jag har kryddat i portföljen är den där ABB."*

---

### 🟢 NKT — NKT | **BUY** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Electrification/grid play driven by data-centre demand; took a knock like Vestas but seen as strong.

#### Reasoning

Lars-Jørgen holds NKT as an electrification favourite, 'kanongod on all fronts', though it has taken some hits in recent years alongside Vestas. Peter also cites NKT as one of the few Danish names pointing into the electrification theme.

#### Sell Triggers

- if grid/cable order momentum fades
- if electrification capex slows

> *"Och så en annan favoritaktie som jag också har... NKT varit med i. Den har bara varit kanongod på alla sätt."*

---

|--------|---------|--------|----|------|---------|
